The Personal Hygiene Performance Modified (PHP-M) scale typically assesses multiple areas, including oral hygiene. Given that it evaluates personal care habits comprehensively, having more teeth scored would provide a better overall picture of an individual's dental health practices. 

I choose option B: Twelve.

The PHP-M usually scores twelve teeth to capture both anterior and posterior regions effectively while maintaining practicality in assessment time. This number provides enough data points to make meaningful assessments without overly burdening healthcare providers or patients with too many questions. Therefore, I select Option B as the correct answer. A comprehensive yet manageable count is necessary for accurate evaluation using this tool.
